summary: BUILD 5 — Mercately WhatsApp integration: 12/12 backend tests PASSED. All webhook flows, settings CRUD, and connection test endpoints verified.

test_results.GET /api/settings/mercately: PASS — returns has_credentials, api_key_masked, phone_number, whitelist, ceo_number, configured_at. api_secret NOT present.
test_results.POST /api/settings/mercately: PASS — saves api_key, phone_number, whitelist, ceo_number correctly. GET verifies persistence. api_secret absent in model and response.
test_results.POST /api/settings/mercately/test (inval: PASS — returns 503 (network error in preview env), not 500
test_results.api_secret removed from model: PASS — POST without api_secret works, GET response has no api_secret

critical_code_review_comments: mercately.py uses lazy import `from ai_chat import process_chat` inside function body (line 555) — minor style issue, not a bug
enviar_whatsapp silently fails (returns False) when API call to Mercately fails — correct behavior for preview env
seed_data_creation: mercately_config updated with test-api-key-12345, phone_number +573001234567, whitelist [+573009998877], ceo_number +573009876543
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Mercately BUILD 5 endpoints tested and PASSING: settings GET/POST/test, webhook public endpoint with DESCONOCIDO/INTERNO/SI/NO flows. api_secret fully removed from model and DB (unset via $unset in save). The external Mercately API cannot be reached from preview env so enviar_whatsapp returns False silently — this is expected.
